evals.prompt: Build a Kortix pitch deck — 6 slides introducing our coding-agent platform that runs in your own repo.
evals.expected_output: An on-brand .pptx generated with pptxgenjs, run through repair.py + office/validate.py until it passes, and visually QA'd. Should read /product-marketing.md and ../internal-comms for copy and ../brand-guidelines for visuals first.
assertions: Reads product-marketing.md (or fallbacks) and ../brand-guidelines/SKILL.md before building.
Generates a .pptx via pptxgenjs using 6-char hex colors with no '#' prefix.
Uses Kortix neutrals plus exactly one accent (e.g. 2B91F7), not a rainbow.
Runs scripts/repair.py then office/validate.py (from inside office/) and iterates until 'All validations PASSED!'.
Composites the Kortix symbol top-left rather than generating or drawing a logo.
Avoids AI-slide hallmarks: no accent underline under titles, no colored side borders, no gradient fills.

evals.prompt: Here's deck.pptx from a client template — swap it to Kortix branding and fix the wording on slides 2 and 4.
evals.expected_output: Enters the editing flow: unpack → edit XML → clean → pack --original, recolored to Kortix tokens, validated, and visually checked.
assertions: Uses office/unpack.py and office/pack.py (run from office/) rather than regenerating from scratch.
Recolors to Kortix neutrals + one accent and recolors the logo to the surface instead of redrawing it.
Removes template hallmarks (accent underlines, colored side borders, gradients) during the recolor.
Validates the result (pack.py --original or office/validate.py) and runs visual QA before delivering.
Edits each slide{N}.xml as an independent file and keeps one <a:p> per logical item.

evals.prompt: Pull all the text out of this quarterly.pptx so I can paste it into an email.
evals.expected_output: Uses markitdown to extract text (and optionally thumbnail.py for a visual grid); does not regenerate or edit the file.
assertions: Runs `python -m markitdown quarterly.pptx` to extract the content.
Does not modify or regenerate the source file for a read-only extraction task.
Returns the extracted text in a usable form.

evals.prompt: I generated slides with pptxgenjs but PowerPoint says the file needs repair. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Runs repair.py (which fixes phantom slideMaster entries, ZIP directory entries, and presentation.xml element order) then office/validate.py to confirm schema validity.
assertions: Runs scripts/repair.py on the .pptx.
Knows raw pptxgenjs output is not schema-valid until repaired (notesMasterIdLst emitted out of order).
Confirms the fix with office/validate.py run from inside the office/ directory.
Iterates fix-and-verify until validation passes.

evals.prompt: Make me a single square social graphic announcing our launch.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is a standalone image, not a slide deck, and defers to the kortix-image skill rather than producing a .pptx.
assertions: Does not generate a .pptx for a single standalone image request.
Defers to ../kortix-image/SKILL.md for social/brand image generation.
